jobs.title: Treatment Center Director/Program Director
jobs.description:
BAART Programs is looking for an accountable, process oriented and efficient leader of operations for our Opiate Treatment Program. A great Treatment Center Director leads a diverse team of nurses, counselors, and auxiliary staff to understand the needs of patients undergoing treatment for opioid addiction while maintaining responsibility for daily operations and performance of the clinic.

Essential Duties & Responsibilities:

Responsible for the operation & performance of the Opiate Treatment Program (OTP) clinic
Manages OTP clinic operations to budgeted/planned results
Participates in the interviewing, hiring, training of clinic staff
Evaluates, manages, counsels and terminates subordinate personnel
Provides direction to clinic personnel and relays corporate mandates, goals and objectives to field personnel
Works closely with staff via regular supervision to ensure the completion of performance goals
Communicates with and insures compliance with the various regulatory bodies (SAMHSA, DEA, CARF, JCAHO, HIPAA, etc.); manages preparation of various federal, state & local applications, filings, licensure, permits, approvals, etc.
Identifies cost-saving opportunities, operational efficiencies, etc. and implements
Assists with partnering opportunities, linkage/support agreements and other critical relationships to drive census growth & meet regulatory requirements
Develops, monitors and provides reports on key performance indicators of clinic measures and reacts accordingly
Works closely with OTP Medical Director, Psychiatric consultant as applicable, OTP Compliance Manager and QA/QI activities in a variety of areas including, but not limited to – compliance with federal & state rules, laws & regulations; compliance with CARF / JCAHO, performance improvement initiatives, productivity goals, quality patient care, customer service, operational enhancement, growth of patient census, contractual requirements, standardization & conformity, best practices and plans for continuous improvement
Becomes familiar with OTP federal & state laws, regulations, etc. and the application of such
Becomes familiar with CARF/JCAHO standards and the application of such
Responsible for maintenance & updating of policy & procedure manual and clinic operations manuals and procedures
Serves as a clinic resource and provides or arranges for clinic training as requested/necessary
Maintains patient, employee and company confidentiality

Development Responsibilities:

Participates in community relations, education and development activities to drive and maintain census
Identifies and implements tactical steps to increase and retain census
Works with clinic team to insure operations are prepared to handle increased census
Participates in political activities to keep company connected within appropriate circles such as local government board meetings, MTP advisory board meetings, city/county administrator meetings, ADAA meetings, etc.
Leads effort to identify, hire & train personnel with cultural diversity to meet the future clinic needs based on patient demographics – identifies cultural community leaders and networks
Participation in the overall Company Performance Improvement Process
Familiar with standards required by Board of Health, OSHA, etc. and the application of such
Assists as requested with marketing programs & literature development
Other duties as assigned

Qualifications:

Multiple years of experience as an administrator of a clinical program
Bachelor's degree (in business administration or related) from an accredited college or university
Understanding of clinic operations, with significant amount of time working in the field of substance abuse
Understanding of HIPAA, Federal, State & CARF standards & regulations
Demonstrated organizational and leadership skills with the ability to supervise and manage personnel
Strategic thinker to be able to recommend alternative solutions, execute and monitor
Self-starter, able to work autonomously and generate ideas and benefits for the Company
Customer service focused, eager and energetic
Excellent interpersonal and communication skills
Satisfactory drug screen and criminal background check.

Salary Range:

Salary ranges from $77,945.40 - $90,000.00 annualized.

The salary of the candidate(s) selected for this role will be set based on a variety of factors, including but not limited to, experience, education, specialty and training.

BayMark offers excellent benefits:

401K match
Medical, Dental, Vision Insurance
Accident Injury, Hospital Indemnity and Critical Illness Plans
Company paid Short & Long Term Disability
Company paid Basic Life Insurance
Paid Time Off
Bereavement Leave
Flexible Sick Time
Employee Referral Program

Total compensation goes beyond the value on the paycheck. Please consider the total compensation package by contacting us at BayMark Health Services for more information.

Here is what you can expect from us:

BAART Programs, a progressive substance abuse treatment organization is committed to the highest quality of patient care in a comfortable outpatient clinic setting. Our ultimate goal is to address the physical, emotional, and mental aspects of opioid use disorder to help each of our patients achieve long-term recovery and an improved quality of life.

BAART Programs is committed to Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) and to compliance with all Federal, State and local laws that prohibit employment discrimination on the basis of race, color, age, natural origin, ethnicity, religion, gender, pregnancy, marital status, sexual orientation, citizenship, genetic disposition, disability or veteran’s status or any other classification protected by State/Federal laws.

jobs.title: Development Director
jobs.description:
Position Title:
Development Director
Reports to:
Executive Director
Employment Type:
Salaried, full-time
Compensation: $
150,000
Location:
Remote

About MPU

More Perfect Union is an advocacy, journalism, and education organization with a mission to build power for the working class. They speak directly to young, working class Americans by reporting on the real struggles and challenges of people from a working class perspective and connecting those problems to potential solutions.

Since launching in February 2021, More Perfect Union’s Emmy award-winning coverage has garnered over 6 million followers and subscribers and become the fastest growing, left-of-center YouTube channel. Their work seeks to embolden and empower the working class to engage in building our democracy for the betterment of all working Americans.

About the Position

The Development Director will play a central role in supporting More Perfect Union’s next phase of growth. The person will lead expansion of the development function of one of the fastest growing nonprofit media organizations in the country at a critical time. This is a highly collaborative role, working closely with the Executive Director, Chief Operating Officer, and Editor-in-Chief to advance major donor and foundation fundraising in support of sustainability growing More Perfect Union.

This person will advance the organization's major gift fundraising strategy and directly influence the development of organizational priorities and long-term financial goals. They will also strengthen development systems, and create materials and messaging that are aligned with organizational priorities.

The ideal candidate will have a deep passion for the mission of More Perfect Union and be able to effectively translate that passion into the major gifts fundraising work of the organization. They should be both strategic and detail oriented, comfortable managing multiple workstreams, a great communicator and writer, and excited to help grow major gifts development functions within a fast growing media organization.

Core Responsibilities:

In close partnership with Senior Leadership, plan and lead the organization’s major gift fundraising strategy, including donor prioritization, portfolio management, and donor engagement.
Maintain portfolio of major donors, engage donors in written communication, phone calls, visits and relationship maintenance
Perform regular prospecting for the organization
Manage and support outreach to major donors and foundations, including research, preparation of materials, donor briefings, meeting follow up, and ongoing relationship tracking
Lead the creation, maintenance, and regular updating of written development materials, including pitch decks, donor briefs, grant narratives, and background documents
Develop innovative major donor engagement approaches, solicitation timing, and stewardship priorities to help More Perfect Union continue to expand
Exercise discretion in advising senior leadership on fundraising and development goals, with fundraising projections
Track development activity and progress toward fundraising goals, and help identify opportunities to strengthen and expand support
Scheduling donor meetings as needed
Collaborate across teams to ensure fundraising materials accurately reflect editorial priorities, organizational impact, and future plans
Manage multiple projects simultaneously and meet expected deadlines
Maintain high standard of work in a nimble, fast paced environment
Travel as required for visits
Perform other duties as assigned

Experience/Qualifications:

At least 5 years experience in nonprofit or political fundraising/development at progressive organizations, advocacy groups, or media organizations with at least 3 years experience soliciting major gifts.
Strong writing and editing skills, with experience creating donor facing materials such as decks, proposals, or memos
Experience in principal management
Experience with major donor and foundation fundraising, including relationship management, outreach support and solicitation
Highly organized and detail oriented, with the ability to manage multiple projects and deadlines simultaneously
Familiarity with SalesForce or other CRM system
Comfortable working closely with senior leadership and supporting high level fundraising efforts
Ability to be nimble, hustle and flexible
Excitement about building systems and processes in a fast paced, growing organization
Commitment to More Perfect Union’s mission and values

At More Perfect Union we strive to increase diversity, equity, inclusion and justice in all elements of our work. We recruit, employ, train, compensate, and promote regardless of race, religion, creed, national origin, ancestry, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, gender identity (including gender nonconformity and status as a transgender individual), age, physical or mental disability, post-secondary educational status, citizenship, genetic information, past, current, or prospective service in the uniformed services, or any other characteristic protected under applicable federal, state, or local law. We strongly encourage members of historically underrepresented and marginalized communities to apply.

The preferred application deadline is August 21, 2026.This search is being conducted by Naomi Roth-Gaudette and Joe Ready at NRG Consulting Group. Please reach out to jobs@nrgconsultinggroup.org if you have any questions.

Show less

Seniority level

Director

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Strategy/Planning, Management, and Business Development

Industries

Internet News, Civic and Social Organizations, and Non-profit Organizations

jobs.title: Chief of Staff to the CEO (Execution) – Contract
jobs.description:
Reports to the CEO

Remote (US), Occasional travel to Southern California
Early career welcome

About Korrus

Korrus is transforming the way people interact with light. We are the leader in human-centric lighting, built on more than 500 patents in spectral and circadian technology, and pioneers in Human Light Interaction, the next frontier in human health and wellness.

That work runs on three fronts:

Ecosense. A globally known and respected leader in architectural and commercial lighting, and the business now bringing circadian technology into the spaces where people work, heal, learn, and gather.
OIO. Our consumer brand, bringing circadian lighting into the home: biologically aligned light, the right spectrum at the right time of day, to support how people wake, focus, and rest.
Everything else in Human Light Interaction. We are pushing the limits of what light can do for human health and performance, and we own the intellectual property to do it.

Behind the products is a real, complex company: engineering, manufacturing, a global supply chain, a commercial sales organization, a consumer business, and the operations that tie it all together. We run it as a horizontal organization with AI as the backbone. We care far less about the functional title you have held than about whether you can operate across all of it.

The role

This is a broad, high-ownership role in the Office of the CEO. You are the person any member of the leadership team can point at a problem. You take the direction, translate it into a plan with an owner, a date, and a definition of done, then make sure it happens.

You will report to the CEO and work daily with the CEO, CFO, and CIO of a global business. In a single week you can work with product development, engineering, manufacturing, supply chain, finance, a consumer brand going to market, and you will be deploying AI across all of it. You will have a direct mandate from the CEO with full visibility.

You do not need years of experience, and you do not need to know lighting. You need range, an unusual learning rate, and the nerve to hold a room accountable to what it committed to.

Who We Are Looking For

This is a deliberate profile. If you recognize yourself here, we want to talk to you.

You intend to start a company. This seat is the fastest honest way to see how an entire business actually works: the P&L, the factory, the channel, the product, the decisions and their consequences. Some of the best people in this role use it as the last stop before founding something. We would rather hire that person than someone looking for a safe title for the next ten years.
You are looking at entrepreneurship through acquisition. You want to buy and run a real business. Come learn how one is actually operated, from the people operating it, before you go do it with your own capital on the line.
You just finished an MBA and you are better than your offer list. We do not care who interviewed you or who did not. We care what you can do here, and we will find out fast.
You did your two years in investment banking or consulting. You are tired of building the recommendation and never seeing it land. Here you own the execution, and the outcome has your name on it.

What every one of those people has in common is capacity, acumen, and a willingness to earn their stripes.

What You Get Out Of It

A direct line to the CEO and CFO of a global business, and a seat in the rooms where the calls actually get made.
Range across executive leadership, finance, accounting, innovation, engineering, product development, supply chain, manufacturing, operations, and a consumer brand launching into the market. Not a rotation through them one at a time: all of it, at once.
The mandate to deploy AI across an entire organization, not to run a pilot in one corner of it.
Real operators to learn from, in an industry that is being rebuilt around science that did not exist a decade ago.
Two years of this and you will know how a company is actually run. That is the trade, and it is the reason to take this job over a safer one.

What This Role Is Not

Not an executive assistant with a better title. You will not manage calendars, book travel, or take notes for other people.
Not a ceremonial chief of staff. You are measured on outcomes that shipped, not meetings you attended.
Not a product, engineering, or design decision maker. You hold the line on what was committed to and when.
Not a specialist seat. If you want to go deep in one function and be judged on that function alone, this is the wrong role.

How We See Your First Few Weeks

We expect you to start with a learning phase: time to understand the business, meet the people whose work you will be driving, and find the first problem worth owning. Expect to ask a great many questions, and expect us to make time for them. We would rather you scope the work properly than rush to a fixed timeline. You will own something real early, and grow quickly into driving work across the whole company.

What You’ll Do

Take direction from any member of the leadership team and drive it to completion across whatever functions it touches.
Turn a one-line intent into a plan: scope, owner, date, definition of done, and the two or three things that will actually make it late.
Run the operating cadence: program status, gate reviews, commitments, and the follow-through in between. Surface slippage early, not at the deadline.
Diagnose why work is late, and go find the answer at the source rather than passing along the explanation you were given.
Build AI-assisted and agentic workflows that do this work at a scale one person could not, and keep raising what a single operator can cover.
Prepare the analysis and the materials that leadership, and sometimes the board, make decisions from.

What We’re Looking For

Range. Enough engineering, finance, operations, and commercial sense to hold your own in any of those rooms and not be lost. Depth in one of them is a bonus, not the requirement.
Capacity. You absorb an unreasonable amount, quickly, and you connect it across disciplines rather than filing it by department.
Energy. This job is demanding, and the people who do it well want it that way.
Fearlessness. You will ask the leadership team the question you think sounds dumb, in the room, in front of everyone. You will pick up the phone and call the person who has not answered your fifth email.
Initiative. Told to get over there, you figure out the route. You do not wait for the door-by-door version.
Business acumen, including real comfort with a P&L and the numbers underneath it.
Genuine fluency with modern AI tools and agentic workflows, and the taste to know when the output is not good enough. You want to learn more, not less.
Two to four years in banking, consulting, private equity, or high-growth operations, or an engineering background. A recent MBA is a strong fit; an engineer who then did an MBA is our ideal.
Where you went to school matters far less to us than whether you have something to prove.
No lighting industry experience required, and none expected.

How We Evaluate People Here

We assess every person at Korrus on four dimensions. We will use them on you in the interview, in the exercise, and every month you are here, so they are worth understanding before you apply.

Energy. The effort you actually exert. It is the easiest dimension to over-index on, and it is a floor, not a ceiling.
Attitude. How you show up. Not whether you participate, but whether your presence makes the work better. The standard is proactive contribution: receive what is in front of you, build on it, and move the work forward through the ambiguity.
Competency. What you know, and increasingly the judgment to hold work up against the right quality bar and know what good looks like, whether that work is yours, your team’s, or produced by AI.
Capacity. The rate at which you acquire competency and direct it. This is the dimension AI compounds: the more you master the tools, the more leverage you get, and the more leverage you get, the faster you learn.

Attitude and Capacity are the entrance requirements, at any level, on any day. Competency determines where and how fast you contribute. Energy determines whether you are carrying your share of the load. For this seat we are hiring early in the journey, which sets the profile: high Energy, high Attitude, high Capacity, and competency in our business that you will build here rather than bring with you.

Strong pluses

You have started something of your own, or intend to.
You have built AI-assisted or agentic workflows that other people came to rely on.
Experience across a hardware business: engineering, manufacturing, supply chain, or the commercial side of one.

Logistics And Compensation

Contract Role
$11,000-$15,000/Monthly DOE
Remote (US), with meaningful overlap with Pacific working hours and occasional travel to Southern California for in-person working sessions.
Reports to the CEO, with a standing mandate to take direction from any member of the leadership team.

To be considered

Send us your resume and, in two or three sentences, tell us about a time you were responsible for something you had no authority over and got it done anyway. We are especially interested in what you did with AI to make it faster. Finalists complete a short, paid exercise built from a real problem of the kind this job produces every week, so we can see how you work.

Korrus is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We evaluate qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic.

This position is not eligible for current or future visa sponsorship.

jobs.title: Director of Communications, Mentor Worldwide
jobs.description:
At Johnson & Johnson, we believe health is everything. Our strength in healthcare innovation empowers us to build a world where complex diseases are prevented, treated, and cured, where treatments are smarter and less invasive, and solutions are personal. Through our expertise in Innovative Medicine and MedTech, we are uniquely positioned to innovate across the full spectrum of healthcare solutions today to deliver the breakthroughs of tomorrow, and profoundly impact health for humanity. Learn more at jnj.com

As guided by Our Credo, Johnson & Johnson is responsible to our employees who work with us throughout the world. We provide an inclusive work environment where each person is considered as an individual. At Johnson & Johnson, we respect the diversity and dignity of our employees and recognize their merit.

Job Function

Communications & Corporate/External Affairs

Job Sub Function

Product Communications

Job Category

Professional

All Job Posting Locations:

Irvine, California, United States of America, Raritan, New Jersey, United States of America, Santa Clara, California, United States of America

Job Description

We are searching for the best talent to join our team as a Director of Communications, Mentor Worldwide. This is a hybrid in-person role with a preferred location in Irvine, California although Raritan, NJ and Santa Clara, CA may be considered.

The Director of Communications, Mentor Worldwide will serve as a valued counselor to the global platform leadership team while also partnering with a cross-functional teams and teams in regions to advance organizational priorities, drive strategy alignment, and achieve commercial goals. This role will be required to build a deep understanding of the business, technology development and strategy, and market opportunities that will inform an integrated global communications and marcomms plan.

This will be a highly visible role as you will work in close collaboration with functional colleagues around the world, including Global Strategic Marketing, Brand Marketing, Region Commercial, MedTech global communications, Human Resources, Legal, Johnson & Johnson leadership, and more.

This role requires a forward-thinking, strategic leader who challenges the status quo in aesthetics and reconstructive surgery space. This leader must be comfortable working in dynamic, fast-paced environments with multiple internal and external partners to consider. A successful candidate will also demonstrate strong executive presence and the ability to thoughtfully advise top executives. The position will lead a team of communicators and independently lead project teams of agency professionals on both an ongoing and as needed basis.

Education

Bachelor’s degree in Communications, Journalism, Public Relations, Public Affairs, or closely related field (required).

Experience

Minimum 10 years leading communications in a corporate, government, or agency environment (required).
Experience in healthcare and/or consumer health communications (required).
Demonstrated success in content strategy, media relations, digital and social channels, and integrated communications campaigns.
Strong track record of independently driving high‑impact communication programs across multiple audiences.

Skills

Exceptional writing and presentation development skills with strong attention to detail.
Strong influence, relationship‑building, and executive‑level communication capabilities.
Deep understanding of media analytics, content strategy, and brand and marketing communications

Johnson & Johnson is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, national origin, disability, protected veteran status or other characteristics protected by federal, state or local law. We actively seek qualified candidates who are protected veterans and individuals with disabilities as defined under VEVRAA and Section 503 of the Rehabilitation Act.

Johnson & Johnson is committed to providing an interview process that is inclusive of our applicants’ needs. If you are an individual with a disability and would like to request an accommodation, external applicants please contact us via https://www.jnj.com/contact-us/careers , internal employees contact AskGS to be directed to your accommodation resource.

Required Skills

Brand Identity, Communications Measurement, Corporate Communications Design, Corporate Communications Management

Preferred Skills

Agility Jumps, Brand Recognition, Competitive Landscape Analysis, Corporate Management, Cultural Competence, Customer Experience Management, External Communication, Leadership, Product Knowledge, Relationship Building, Stakeholder Engagement, Tactical Planning, Technical Credibility

The anticipated base pay range for this position is :

$150,000.00 - $258,750.00

For Bay Area

$172,000.00 - $297,850.00

Additional Description For Pay Transparency

Subject to the terms of their respective plans, employees are eligible to participate in the Company’s consolidated retirement plan (pension) and savings plan (401(k)).

This position is eligible to participate in the Company’s long-term incentive program.

Benefits

Subject to the terms of their respective policies and date of hire, employees are eligible for the following time off benefits:

Vacation –120 hours per calendar year

Sick time - 40 hours per calendar year; for employees who reside in the State of Colorado –48 hours per calendar year; for employees who reside in the State of Washington –56 hours per calendar year

Holiday pay, including Floating Holidays –13 days per calendar year

Work, Personal and Family Time - up to 40 hours per calendar year

Parental Leave – 480 hours within one year of the birth/adoption/foster care of a child

Bereavement Leave – 240 hours for an immediate family member: 40 hours for an extended family member per calendar year

Caregiver Leave – 80 hours in a 52-week rolling period10 days

Volunteer Leave – 32 hours per calendar year

Military Spouse Time-Off – 80 hours per calendar year

For additional general information on Company benefits, please go to: - https://www.careers.jnj.com/employee-benefits

jobs.title: Director of Communications, Reputation
jobs.description:
Our Mission

At Palo Alto Networks®, we’re united by a shared mission—to protect our digital way of life. We thrive at the intersection of innovation and impact, solving real-world problems with cutting-edge technology and bold thinking. Here, everyone has a voice, and every idea counts. If you’re ready to do the most meaningful work of your career alongside people who are just as passionate as you are, you’re in the right place.

Who We Are

In order to be the cybersecurity partner of choice, we must trailblaze the path and shape the future of our industry. This is something our employees work at each day and is defined by our values: Disruption, Collaboration, Execution, Integrity, and Inclusion. We weave AI into the fabric of everything we do and use it to augment the impact every individual can have. If you are passionate about solving real-world problems and ideating beside the best and the brightest, we invite you to join us!

We believe collaboration thrives in person. That’s why most of our teams work from the office full time, with flexibility when it’s needed. This model supports real-time problem-solving, stronger relationships, and the kind of precision that drives great outcomes.

Job Summary

We are looking for a talented, strategic, and unflappable leader to head Palo Alto Networks reputation communications. You will join our high-agency, low-ego communications team to architect and execute our global crisis communications, issues management, and proactive brand protection strategies. The ideal candidate is a seasoned corporate communications expert who thrives in high-stakes environments, values deep cross-functional relationships, and understands how to protect and enhance corporate reputation in the fast-moving enterprise technology and cybersecurity landscape.

In this role, you are the guardian of our corporate narrative during critical moments. From navigating complex regulatory landscapes and data security issues to driving rapid-response messaging, you will bring steady leadership and sharp strategic instincts to ensure Palo Alto Networks maintains trust with customers, partners, media, and the public. This position plays a critical role within a company that deeply values its communications team and relies on us to navigate ambiguity with clarity and integrity.

Your Impact

Crisis & Issues Management: Lead the global strategy and execution for high-stakes, fast-moving communication issues, ensuring rapid, accurate, and transparent messaging across all external audiences.
Reputation Stewardship: Proactively identify, monitor, and mitigate potential reputational risks before they escalate, safeguarding Palo Alto Networks' brand equity in the global market.
Cross-Functional Alignment: Work skillfully across the company—partnering deeply with Legal, Information Security, Product, Public Policy, and Executive Leadership—to synthesize complex technical or legal situations into clear, human-centric communications.
Media Relations & Spokespersonship: Serve as a primary point of contact and trusted advisor for high-profile media inquiries. Build and maintain strong relationships with top-tier business, tech, and security journalists.
Preparedness & Governance: Own, update, and scale our global crisis communications playbooks, FAQs, and response frameworks, ensuring internal stakeholders are trained and ready for rapid deployment.
Strategic Messaging: Draft precise, high-impact statements, talking points, and executive communications that maintain corporate transparency while managing legal and regulatory boundaries.
Steely Execution: Act as the steady anchor for the communications team during times of heightened external attention, managing multiple moving pieces with speed, precision, and absolute discretion.

Qualifications

Enterprise Tech Expertise: 12–15+ years of experience in crisis communications, corporate PR, or issues management, with a strong background in enterprise software, cloud technology, or cybersecurity.
High Agency, Low Ego: A proven track record of taking accountability and driving definitive outcomes during critical events, while maintaining a collaborative, supportive, and grounded team-first attitude.
Battle-Tested Leader: Demonstrated experience managing high-profile corporate crises and turning complex, high-pressure situations into calm, structured execution.
Master Communicator: Exceptional writing, editing, and verbal skills, with a proven ability to translate highly technical milestones, legal constraints, and security events into clear, consumer-forward prose.
Relationship Driven: A natural ability to build deep trust quickly with senior executives, legal counsel, and engineering teams, guiding alignment when viewpoints diverge.
Media Savvy: Extensive experience engaging directly with tier-one media outlets and comfortably serving as a public corporate spokesperson when required.
Location: Ability to work out of our Santa Clara office in our hybrid model.

Compensation Disclosure

The compensation offered for this position will depend on qualifications, experience, and work location. For candidates who receive an offer at the posted level, the starting base salary (for non-sales roles) or base salary + commission target (for sales/com-missioned roles) is expected to be the annual range listed below. The offered compensation may also include restricted stock units and a bonus. A description of our employee benefits may be found here.

/yr

Our Commitment

We’re trailblazers that dream big, take risks, and challenge cybersecurity’s status quo. It’s simple: we can’t accomplish our mission without diverse teams innovating, together.

We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations for all qualified individuals with a disability. If you require assistance or accommodation due to a disability or special need, please contact us at accommodations@paloaltonetworks.com.

Palo Alto Networks is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity in our workplace, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran status, race, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or other legally protected characteristics.

All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

Is role eligible for Immigration Sponsorship? No. Please note that we will not sponsor applicants for work visas for this position.

jobs.title: Executive Director, Creative
jobs.description:
WHY DEPT®?

We are a Growth Invention company built to help the world’s most ambitious brands grow faster. Operating at the intersection of technology and marketing, we create what is next by pioneering ideas, acting fast, and moving further because standing still just is not in our DNA.

We are drawn to people who stay curious, move with intent, and never stop inventing. Our culture runs on three values: better together, relentlessly curious, and get sh*t done. It is how we work, how we grow, and how we make things that matter.

At DEPT®, you will find the freedom to explore, the space to collaborate, and the trust to make a real impact for our clients, for each other, and for the world we are helping to build.

THE ROLE:

The role of Executive Director, Creative is an integral part of DEPT® leadership and provides oversight of all areas of the DEPT®’s creative work for an industry-leading AI client to ensure that large-scale creative solutions are developed and meet and even exceed the client's needs. You are not only a master of the creative and design process, including a strong understanding of performance media, but also of short and long-term planning, the setting of goals, developing and driving the company’s creative vision, and evolving and improving the company’s design aesthetic for an industry-leading AI client. This also involves ensuring that the organization’s talent is in place and performing at a level that will continue to improve and grow the business. You are leadership-focused, detail-oriented, excellent at multitasking, hyper-focused on efficiency, and eager to implement change and make things come to life.

This special someone will regularly exercise discretion and independent judgment and will lead the company's creative and design teams in the development of digital experiences, e-commerce, and content for an industry-leading AI client, as well as lifestyle and technology categories, to create solutions that deeply connect with people. You love positioning brands to matter in culture, and you possess a strong design intuition, expertise in user experiences across the web, and strong strategic thinking skills. You will work closely with others on the leadership team to develop and implement the overall strategy for the organization to ensure the continued health of the company. You strive to assist all parts of the organization in making good business decisions, and you take pride in the success of the company and its employees. You will be dealing with multiple personality types, and need to be able to adapt based on the person you are working with and communicating with.

In your role, you will report to the SVP Creative, who drives the overall strategy and direction for the company. You will support the strategic goals of the company by focusing on and assisting with meeting the short and long-term objectives of the organization.

WHAT YOU’LL DO:

As part of the DEPT®’s embedded client leadership team, assist in formulating the company’s future direction for an industry-leading AI client, strategy, vision, and overall business plan.
Directly supervise the Creative team across the organization and work to develop them to ensure a high level of performance.
Responsible for ensuring that the company hires, retains, and develops the best creative talent and continues to provide a culture that emphasizes collaboration, professional growth, and a strong desire to provide the great products and customer service that the organization is known for.
Oversee and lead digital experiences and solutions from conception through implementation.
Partner with cross-functional client teams to define project strategy and translate strategy to the development of creative concepts and content ideas.
Help to develop creative ideas that can extend across all digital platforms to drive results for our clients.
Ensure that the company delivers services and products that always exceed the clients’ expectations, foreseeing any issues and developing solutions to prevent future disruptions to client satisfaction.
Contribute to new business efforts, including networking, vision, strategy, creative output, and agency representation.
Develop and deliver engaging presentations for internal and external audiences.
Work across all departments of B/D® and other stakeholders, including the clients if needed, to achieve the desired results and meet company objectives.
Provide leadership to the team, including putting the client and the team before yourself.
Responsible for bringing a point-of-view with fresh perspectives to the team.
Ability to navigate ambiguous situations and solve problems by being optimistic and using proactive thought processes and skills.
Ensure that projects come in within deadlines and the specified budget.
Identify the decision makers within the client's organization and use excellent interpersonal skills to grow and maintain these relationships.
Work across all departments of DEPT®, the client, and other stakeholders to achieve the desired results and meet the objectives of the company.
Support and grow in the role by actively seeking out and proposing ideas to improve company operations and profitability.
Stay current on industry trends, best practices, and technology.

WHAT YOU BRING:

12+ years of proven track record of leadership in multi-million dollar digital marketing organizations.
Demonstrates excellent leadership, strategic vision, and creative inspiration to foster a cohesive and motivated team from diverse backgrounds and disciplines, with strong interpersonal and relationship-building skills.
Strong knowledge of visual design, user-centered experience design, interaction design, brand development, and the creative process.
Strong understanding of performance media and the iterative process to take learnings and apply them to the creative process.
A love for typography, interaction, traditional graphic design, and technology.
Strong skills in modern design and prototyping tools.
A solid point-of-view and a collaborative nature.
A diverse sense of taste with the knowledge necessary to apply this to various challenges.
Passion for hardware, AI, and technology products.
Ability to be self-motivated to learn and stay current with emerging technologies, as well as the ability to teach others.
A long history of building strong relationships with colleagues, vendors/contractors, and business partners.
An open and curious mind in all areas of creative and design work, and the ability to remain positive even in the most challenging of situations.
Possesses strategic thinking and high-level problem-solving abilities to assess and address current and future company needs.
Exceptional communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to communicate information, whether technical or non-technical, to clients and employees clearly and concisely.
Excellent organizational skills and a very high level of attention to detail, including tracking, recording, documenting, and saving all information, processes, and procedures, and searching for and proposing process efficiencies.
High level of ability to set goals and priorities in a constantly changing environment and to work under tight deadlines.
Excellent public speaking skills with the ability to develop plans, build presentations, and comfortably present them professionally and effectively to those outside and within the organization.
Ability to maintain a positive attitude even in the most challenging of situations.
Rigorous analytical skills with the ability to review a large amount of information quickly and summarize it in a very organized and efficient manner.
Comfort navigating a complex organization with multiple stakeholders.
Communicates effectively one-on-one and in large groups.
Ability to travel regularly for domestic and international travel to maintain new business initiatives and build client relationships.

WHAT DO WE OFFER?

We are a collaborative, open-door, best-idea-wins environment that fosters personal and company growth and has fun doing it. Here is a snapshot of our benefits package:

Healthcare, Dental, and Vision coverage
401k plan, plus matching
PTO
Paid Company Holidays
Parental Leave

WE SUPPORT YOU BEING YOU:

DEPT® is an equal opportunity employer (EOE). We believe our work is its best when our people feel safe and free to be themselves. We’re committed to an inclusive and barrier-free recruitment and selection process and workplace, regardless of anyone’s identity, background, beliefs, lifestyle or disabilities. So tell us about any accommodations you need. It’s totally confidential and only used to make sure you feel fully supported at every step.

DEPT® participates in E-Verify, meaning your Form I-9 information will be sent to the federal government to confirm you are authorized to work in the U.S.

We are a B Corp-certified company passionate about purpose-driven work. Our hope is that you can feel good about the contributions DEPT® is making to the world and we always have an open door for your ideas in making the world a better place.

DEPT® is a Growth Invention company built to help the world’s most ambitious brands grow faster. Operating at the intersection of technology and marketing, our 4,000+ specialists deliver growth invention across Brand & Media, Experience, Commerce, CRM, and Technology & Data. We’re 50|50 tech and marketing, partner-led, and first to move. Clients include Google, Lufthansa, Coach, eBay, and OpenAI.

Learn more about DEPT®

DIVERSITY, EQUITY, & INCLUSION

At DEPT®, we take pride in creating an inclusive workplace where everyone has an equal opportunity to thrive. We actively seek to recruit, develop, nurture, and retain talented individuals from diverse backgrounds, with varying skills and perspectives.

Not sure you meet all qualifications? Apply, and let us decide! Research shows that women and members of underrepresented groups tend not to apply for jobs when they think they may not meet every requirement, when in fact they do. We believe in giving everyone a fair chance to shine.

We also encourage you to reach out to us and discuss any reasonable adjustments we can make to support you throughout the recruitment process and your time with us.

Want to know more about our dedication to diversity, equity, and inclusion? Check out our efforts here .

This is remote role, however you must be located within 50 miles of New York, NY (tri-state area is ok) to be considered.

$225,200—$281,500 USD

About MPU

More Perfect Union is an advocacy, journalism, and education organization with a mission to build power for the working class. They speak directly to young, working class Americans by reporting on the real struggles and challenges of people from a working class perspective and connecting those problems to potential solutions.

Since launching in February 2021, More Perfect Union’s Emmy award-winning coverage has garnered over 6 million followers and subscribers and become the fastest growing, left-of-center YouTube channel. Their work seeks to embolden and empower the working class to engage in building our democracy for the betterment of all working Americans.

About the Position

The Development Director will play a central role in supporting More Perfect Union’s next phase of growth. The person will lead expansion of the development function of one of the fastest growing nonprofit media organizations in the country at a critical time. This is a highly collaborative role, working closely with the Executive Director, Chief Operating Officer, and Editor-in-Chief to advance major donor and foundation fundraising in support of sustainability growing More Perfect Union.

This person will advance the organization's major gift fundraising strategy and directly influence the development of organizational priorities and long-term financial goals. They will also strengthen development systems, and create materials and messaging that are aligned with organizational priorities.

The ideal candidate will have a deep passion for the mission of More Perfect Union and be able to effectively translate that passion into the major gifts fundraising work of the organization. They should be both strategic and detail oriented, comfortable managing multiple workstreams, a great communicator and writer, and excited to help grow major gifts development functions within a fast growing media organization.

Core Responsibilities:

In close partnership with Senior Leadership, plan and lead the organization’s major gift fundraising strategy, including donor prioritization, portfolio management, and donor engagement.
Maintain portfolio of major donors, engage donors in written communication, phone calls, visits and relationship maintenance
Perform regular prospecting for the organization
Manage and support outreach to major donors and foundations, including research, preparation of materials, donor briefings, meeting follow up, and ongoing relationship tracking
Lead the creation, maintenance, and regular updating of written development materials, including pitch decks, donor briefs, grant narratives, and background documents
Develop innovative major donor engagement approaches, solicitation timing, and stewardship priorities to help More Perfect Union continue to expand
Exercise discretion in advising senior leadership on fundraising and development goals, with fundraising projections
Track development activity and progress toward fundraising goals, and help identify opportunities to strengthen and expand support
Scheduling donor meetings as needed
Collaborate across teams to ensure fundraising materials accurately reflect editorial priorities, organizational impact, and future plans
Manage multiple projects simultaneously and meet expected deadlines
Maintain high standard of work in a nimble, fast paced environment
Travel as required for visits
Perform other duties as assigned

Experience/Qualifications:

At least 5 years experience in nonprofit or political fundraising/development at progressive organizations, advocacy groups, or media organizations with at least 3 years experience soliciting major gifts.
Strong writing and editing skills, with experience creating donor facing materials such as decks, proposals, or memos
Experience in principal management
Experience with major donor and foundation fundraising, including relationship management, outreach support and solicitation
Highly organized and detail oriented, with the ability to manage multiple projects and deadlines simultaneously
Familiarity with SalesForce or other CRM system
Comfortable working closely with senior leadership and supporting high level fundraising efforts
Ability to be nimble, hustle and flexible
Excitement about building systems and processes in a fast paced, growing organization
Commitment to More Perfect Union’s mission and values

At More Perfect Union we strive to increase diversity, equity, inclusion and justice in all elements of our work. We recruit, employ, train, compensate, and promote regardless of race, religion, creed, national origin, ancestry, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, gender identity (including gender nonconformity and status as a transgender individual), age, physical or mental disability, post-secondary educational status, citizenship, genetic information, past, current, or prospective service in the uniformed services, or any other characteristic protected under applicable federal, state, or local law. jobs.title: Agency Development Director
jobs.description:
Realize your potential by joining the leading performance-driven advertising company and a proud Great Place to Work® Certified employer in the US

As an Agency Development Director on the Agency Development team, you'll lead Taboola's North American agency development strategy for 2-3 holding companies, working closely with our Director of Agency Development on overall coverage and strategy while operating with autonomy on your book.

You'll own and grow executive relationships at C-suite, EVP and SVP—engaging trading desk leads, programmatic heads, and department leadership—to champion Taboola's value and work hand-in-hand with our sales teams to make agencies true believers in performance advertising beyond search and social.

This role operates upstream of individual campaigns and accounts, focusing on influencing agency strategy, education, and partnership at scale rather than direct deal execution. This is a high-impact, externally facing role for someone who thrives on building influential relationships and turning them into measurable business outcomes.

To Thrive In This Role, You'll Need

12+ years of experience in digital advertising at a top-tier technology or media company, with a strong focus on holding company agency development
Established executive relationships (C-suite, EVP, SVP)at major US agency holding companies and their subsidiaries, spanning multiple practice areas
Deep knowledge of the agency ecosystem — how holding companies, trading desks, and agency brands operate, plan, and buy media
A track record of driving revenue growth and business impact through strategic agency partnerships, rather than direct account-level selling
An ambitious entrepreneurial mindset. A love of building relationships and executing strategy with minimal oversight
Demonstrated experience negotiating or structuring JBPs, MSAs, or similar enterprise-level commercial agreements
Comfort with frequent travel (typically 2+ domestic trips per month for 1-2 nights, with occasional longer trips)

Bonus Points If You Have

Experience working closely with programmatic or performance-focused agency buyers at major US holding companies
Experience repositioning a platform or product that faced a legacy perception challenge in market

How You'll Make An Impact

Own and grow Taboola's relationships with your assigned US agency holding company portfolio, trading desks, and key agency subsidiaries, serving as the primary strategic partner and lead relationship owner
Map and engage agency organizations at executive level—building relationships across trading desk leadership, programmatic heads, and key decision-makers within your portfolio
Drive agency enablement through trainings, workshops, and strategic roadshows—educating stakeholders on Taboola's platform capabilities and positioning Taboola as a core partner across the agency ecosystem
Partner with agencies on cobranded marketing, thought leadership, tentpole activation, and event activations within your portfolio
Collaborate closely with Taboola's sales teams to connect agency relationships to the right opportunities, helping convert agency enthusiasm into measurable revenue outcomes
Lead commercial agreements (e.g., trading agreements, incentive structures, rate cards) from concept to signature, where they meaningfully advance strategic partnerships and revenue growth
Gather market intelligence and relay agency feedback to internal teams — including product, marketing, and GTM — to continuously sharpen Taboola's agency offering
Represent Taboola at key industry events and in the broader agency community, leading the charge to turn around legacy perceptions of Taboola by taking a proactive approach on reeducation

Why Taboola?

About

If you ask Taboolars what they love about working here, they’ll tell you that they’ve been empowered to realize their full potential while growing and learning from and with smart and talented people. They’ll also share more about:

Adam Singolda, Taboola Founder and CEO says; “You can copy anything from another business but you can’t copy a company’s culture.
Well-being: Enjoy comprehensive benefits (health, dental, vision, 401k matching), pre-tax commuter benefits, a fully stocked kitchen, and various gym partnerships.
Flexibility: We offer a hybrid work schedule with 3 days in-office with an option to come in more often if desired.
Work with some of the biggest names: Our publisher partners include Yahoo, Conde Nast, Fox Sports, NBCU, ESPN, CBS, and E! Online. Our advertiser clients include Wells Fargo, Honda, Pinterest, Expedia and Honda.

Ready to realize your potential?

Taboola is an equal opportunity employer and we value diversity in all forms. We are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees and believe such an environment is critical for success. Employment is decided on the basis of qualifications, merit, and business need.

Learn more about #TaboolaLife on LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, X, YouTube, & the Taboola Life Blog.

About Taboola

Taboola empowers businesses to grow through performance advertising technology that goes beyond search and social and delivers measurable outcomes at scale.

Publishers like NBC News, Yahoo, and OEMs such as Samsung, Xiaomi and others use Taboola’s technology to grow audience and revenue, enabling Realize to offer unique data, specialized algorithms, and unmatched scale.

At Johnson & Johnson, we believe health is everything. Our strength in healthcare innovation empowers us to build a world where complex diseases are prevented, treated, and cured, where treatments are smarter and less invasive, and solutions are personal. Through our expertise in Innovative Medicine and MedTech, we are uniquely positioned to innovate across the full spectrum of healthcare solutions today to deliver the breakthroughs of tomorrow, and profoundly impact health for humanity. Learn more at jnj.com

As guided by Our Credo, Johnson & Johnson is responsible to our employees who work with us throughout the world. We provide an inclusive work environment where each person is considered as an individual. At Johnson & Johnson, we respect the diversity and dignity of our employees and recognize their merit.

Job Function

Communications & Corporate/External Affairs

Job Sub Function

Product Communications

Job Category

Professional

All Job Posting Locations:

Irvine, California, United States of America, Raritan, New Jersey, United States of America, Santa Clara, California, United States of America

Job Description

We are searching for the best talent to join our team as a Director of Communications, Mentor Worldwide. This is a hybrid in-person role with a preferred location in Irvine, California although Raritan, NJ and Santa Clara, CA may be considered.

The Director of Communications, Mentor Worldwide will serve as a valued counselor to the global platform leadership team while also partnering with a cross-functional teams and teams in regions to advance organizational priorities, drive strategy alignment, and achieve commercial goals. This role will be required to build a deep understanding of the business, technology development and strategy, and market opportunities that will inform an integrated global communications and marcomms plan.

This will be a highly visible role as you will work in close collaboration with functional colleagues around the world, including Global Strategic Marketing, Brand Marketing, Region Commercial, MedTech global communications, Human Resources, Legal, Johnson & Johnson leadership, and more.

This role requires a forward-thinking, strategic leader who challenges the status quo in aesthetics and reconstructive surgery space. This leader must be comfortable working in dynamic, fast-paced environments with multiple internal and external partners to consider. A successful candidate will also demonstrate strong executive presence and the ability to thoughtfully advise top executives. The position will lead a team of communicators and independently lead project teams of agency professionals on both an ongoing and as needed basis.

Education

Bachelor’s degree in Communications, Journalism, Public Relations, Public Affairs, or closely related field (required).

Experience

Minimum 10 years leading communications in a corporate, government, or agency environment (required).
Experience in healthcare and/or consumer health communications (required).
Demonstrated success in content strategy, media relations, digital and social channels, and integrated communications campaigns.
Strong track record of independently driving high‑impact communication programs across multiple audiences.

Skills

Exceptional writing and presentation development skills with strong attention to detail.
Strong influence, relationship‑building, and executive‑level communication capabilities.
Deep understanding of media analytics, content strategy, and brand and marketing communications

Johnson & Johnson is an Equal Opportunity Employer. The Director of Development provides strategic leadership for City Year Rhode Island's comprehensive fundraising program, driving philanthropic revenue growth and long-term financial sustainability in support of the organization's mission. Reporting to the Executive Director and serving as a member of the Senior Leadership Team, the Director develops and executes an integrated fundraising strategy that includes individual giving, corporate partnerships, foundation and government grants, special events, donor stewardship, and Board engagement. The Director serves as a key external ambassador for the organization, cultivating high-impact relationships with donors, funders, corporate leaders, and community stakeholders while partnering closely with the Executive Director and Board to achieve ambitious fundraising goals.

The Director of Development is responsible for leading and executing City Year Rhode Island's comprehensive fundraising strategy to advance the organization's mission and long-term financial sustainability. jobs.title: Chief Program Officer
jobs.description:
MISSION:
Ellis partners with families to provide high-quality early childhood education and out-of-school programs in a warm, nurturing environment. A recognized leader in our field, we support children across the socio-economic spectrum, treasure diversity in all forms, and celebrate the joy of childhood.

Ellis Early Learning (formerly known as Ellis Memorial) is a vibrant, Boston-based nonprofit with a rich history, founded in 1885. Ellis provides high-quality early education and care for infants, toddlers, and preschool children. Ellis is among the most socio-economically, racially, and culturally diverse organizations serving children in Boston. Through a partnership with Boston Public Schools, it operates four high-performing community-based K-1 classrooms. Ellis operates out of two historic buildings in the South End and a new site in Jamaica Plain. Ellis currently serves over 300 children annually. Learn more about us at www.ellisearlylearning.org.

Position Overview

The Chief Program Officer (CPO) is a senior executive leader responsible for the overall quality, implementation, and continuous improvement of all early learning programs, including infant, toddler, and preschool services. The CPO provides strategic leadership and operational oversight for a multi-site program serving children and families, ensuring excellence in teaching, learning, and family engagement.

This role supervises the administrative leadership team, Center Directors, and the Teaching & Learning team, supporting approximately 80 teachers across 22 classrooms in three sites. The CPO plays a critical role in advancing organizational effectiveness, driving innovation, and leading change management efforts to elevate program quality and outcomes.

The ideal candidate is a visionary, equity-driven leader with deep experience in early childhood education, strong knowledge of regulatory and accreditation systems, and a passion for serving diverse and low-income communities. This leader brings a positive, solutions-oriented mindset and fosters a culture of growth, collaboration, and shared accountability across the organization.

Key Responsibilities

Program Leadership & Strategy

Develop and implement a clear vision for high-quality early childhood programming aligned with the organization’s mission and strategic plan.
Lead continuous quality improvement efforts across all programs, ensuring strong child outcomes and family engagement.
Drive innovation and organizational growth by identifying opportunities to enhance program design, delivery, and impact.
Serve as a key member of the executive leadership team, contributing to organizational strategy and decision-making.
Champion a culture of continuous learning and growth, encouraging teams to embrace challenges, reflect on practice, and strive for ongoing improvement.

Supervision & Team Leadership

Supervise and coach Center Directors, ensuring strong site leadership and consistent program quality across locations.
Oversee the Teaching & Learning team, including curriculum, coaching, and professional development functions.
Build a culture of accountability, collaboration, and continuous learning among program staff.
Foster a positive, inclusive work environment where staff feel supported, valued, and motivated to grow.
Align teams around shared goals and outcomes in service of children and families, ensuring clarity of purpose and collective ownership.
Support leadership development and succession planning for program staff.

Program Quality & Compliance

Ensure full compliance with all applicable regulations, including Massachusetts EEC licensing requirements.
Lead and maintain NAEYC accreditation efforts across programs.
Establish systems for monitoring program quality, including classroom observations, data analysis, and staff evaluation.
Use data to inform decision-making and drive measurable improvements in teaching and learning.

Change Management & Organizational Growth

Lead change management initiatives to strengthen systems, practices, and culture across programs.
Partner with the CEO and leadership team to scale and refine program operations as the organization grows.
Model adaptability, resilience, and a growth mindset in navigating change and supporting teams through transitions.
Champion equity-focused practices that support diverse children, families, and staff.
Foster a forward-thinking, adaptive organization prepared for future opportunities and challenges.

Family & Community Engagement

Ensure programs are culturally responsive and inclusive of diverse and low-income populations.
Strengthen family partnerships and engagement strategies.
Represent the organization with external stakeholders, including funders, community partners, and regulatory bodies.

Qualifications

Required

Master’s degree in Early Childhood Education, Child Development, Education Leadership, or a related field.
Significant leadership experience in early childhood education, including prior experience as a Center Director.
Demonstrated experience supervising leaders (e.g., vice presidents, directors, instructional coaches).
Deep knowledge of Massachusetts EEC regulations and NAEYC accreditation standards.
Proven experience working with diverse and low-income populations.
Strong track record of improving program quality and outcomes.

Preferred

Experience leading multi-site early childhood programs.
Experience managing large teams (50+ staff).
Familiarity with data systems and continuous quality improvement frameworks.

Leadership Competencies

Visionary Leadership: Ability to set and communicate a compelling vision for program excellence.
Change Management & Growth Mindset: Demonstrates optimism, adaptability, and a commitment to continuous improvement; able to lead others through change with clarity and purpose.
Team Alignment & Collaboration: Skilled at bringing diverse teams together around shared goals in service of children and families.
Equity & Inclusion: Deep commitment to culturally responsive practices and serving diverse communities.
People Leadership: Strong coaching, supervision, and team development skills.
Operational Excellence: Ability to balance strategic thinking with strong execution.

Work Environment & Scope

Oversees programs serving infants, toddlers, and preschool-aged children.
Supervises administrative leadership, Center Directors, and Teaching & Learning team.
Supports approximately 80 teachers across 22 classrooms in three sites.
Requires weekly travel between South End and Jamaica Plain.

Why Join Ellis

This is an exciting opportunity for a bold and thoughtful leader to help take a high-quality early learning organization to the next level. The Chief Program Officer will play a pivotal role in shaping the future of programs that have a lasting impact on children, families, and communities.

The purpose of this description is to provide the major responsibilities of the position. It is not to be considered a complete statement of all elements of the work that may be performed.

TO APPLY:
Please email your resume and a brief cover letter outlining your interest to HR@EllisEarlyLearning.Org

jobs.title: Associate Director of Operations
jobs.description:
ABOUT TENACITY

Tenacity is a Boston-based nonprofit organization that empowers under-resourced youth to graduate from high school and succeed in post-secondary education. Since 1999, Tenacity has delivered high-dosage youth enrichment programs integrating literacy, life skills, racket sports, fitness, and social-emotional development to students aged 6–22 across Boston, Worcester, and Chelsea. Tenacity students achieve a 95% high school graduation rate and 75% post-secondary success rate — outcomes that far exceed city and state averages. Diversity, equity, and inclusion are core to everything we do.

POSITION SUMMARY

The Associate Director of Operations is a key operational and data leader reporting to the CEO. This role oversees student data systems and BPS contract/site compliance, while also managing core organizational operations, including insurance, technology, facilities, and non-CFO financial administration.

The role directly supervises the HR Coordinator and Office Administrator, providing systems oversight, tools, and senior guidance, and coordinating external HR and legal support as needed.
This position is well-suited for a detail-oriented, systems-minded professional comfortable working across data, operations, and people functions in a fast-paced, mission-driven environment.

Key Responsibilities

1. Student Data & BPS School Site Support (~30%)

Lead student data collection, management, and reporting across all program sites.
Manage administrative and compliance requirements for Boston Public Schools contracts.
Ensure accurate, consistent, and secure student data practices and systems.
Oversee student data systems, including staff training and support.
Produce reports for program leadership, the CEO, BPS, and other stakeholders.
Support the ACE program team in coordination with the ACE Director.
Identify and resolve data quality issues and enforce data standards.
Provide timely student data for grant reporting and development needs.

2. Insurance & Risk Management (~20%)

Manage all organizational insurance policies, including renewals, audits, payments, and compliance.
Serve as the primary contact for insurance claims, broker communications, and coverage questions.
Maintain organized insurance records and prepare documentation for annual audits.
Monitor policy terms, deadlines, and coverage gaps, recommending updates as needed.
Coordinate with the CFO to ensure accurate insurance payments and financial records.

3. Financial Administration (~5%)

Support annual audits by maintaining accurate operational and financial records.
Oversee billing, invoicing, and financial filings for operational expenses.
Manage organizational accounts outside the CFO’s direct scope.
Review and approve Operations and HR budgets with the HR Coordinator and Office Administrator.
Flag budget variances and spending concerns to the CEO and CFO.

4. Technology & Office Operations (~8%)

Technology Oversight

Manage relationships with external IT consultants and technology vendors.
Oversee technology contracts, purchases, and IT strategy, including cybersecurity and data security.
Ensure staff are onboarded with appropriate technology access in coordination with the Office Administrator and IT consultant.

Office & Facilities Management

Manage Tenacity’s building lease and landlord relationship.
Oversee timely resolution of facilities and maintenance issues.
Ensure office spaces are safe, functional, organized, and well-maintained.
Supervise day-to-day office operations, supplies, and vendor management.

5. Supervision of HR Coordinator & Office Administrator (~8%)

Supervise the HR Coordinator and Office Administrator through regular check-ins, guidance, and performance evaluations.
Ensure both team members have the resources and clarity needed to succeed.
Support professional development and connect staff with relevant training opportunities.
Coordinate HR and Operations functions to prevent gaps and duplication.

6. HR Senior Support (~8%)

Ensure the HR Coordinator has the tools, systems, and policies needed to manage daily HR operations.
Provide guidance on HR questions and decisions while maintaining appropriate ownership of day-to-day functions.
Coordinate between the HR Coordinator and external HR consultant to ensure effective engagement and follow-through.
Engage pro bono employment counsel on employee relations matters as needed and brief the CEO on sensitive issues.
Review and approve major HR policy and Employee Handbook updates before staff communication.

7. Leadership & Organizational Meetings (~10%)

Serve on the Senior Leadership Team, contributing to organizational planning and decision-making.
Represent operations and data functions in cross-departmental planning and staff meetings.
Support planning and execution of major annual events.
Model Tenacity’s values of equity, respect, accountability, and continuous learning.
Support planning and execution of major annual events and Tenacity's Summer Tennis and Reading Program.

8. Organizational Flexibility & Special Projects (~11%)

Respond to unplanned organizational needs as directed by the CEO, including time-sensitive issues that cut across departments.
Support staff communications, events, and special initiatives throughout the year.
Provide cross-functional support during staff absences, transitions, and high-demand periods.

QUALIFICATIONS

Education & Experience

Bachelor's degree required; advanced degree in Education, Public Administration, Data Management, Nonprofit Management, or a related field a plus.
Minimum 4–6 years of progressive experience in nonprofit operations, data coordination, or a closely related field.
Demonstrated experience managing data systems and producing reports— experience with student or program data in an educational nonprofit context strongly preferred.
Experience managing compliance documentation for government contracts (e.g., BPS or similar) a strong plus.
Familiarity with insurance administration, vendor management, and organizational operations.
Prior supervisory experience required.

' Skills & Competencies

Strong data management skills, including quality control and clear reporting for diverse audiences.
Highly organized and detail-oriented, with the ability to manage multiple priorities, deadlines, and compliance requirements.
Proficient in Microsoft 365 (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Teams, SharePoint); experience with student data systems or Salesforce preferred.
Exercises discretion and integrity when handling sensitive student, financial, and personnel information.
Collaborative and relationship-oriented, with the ability to build trust across teams and partners.
Strong written and verbal communication skills, including presenting data and operational updates to leadership.
Self-directed and resourceful problem solver who drives improvements with minimal oversight.
Committed to Tenacity’s mission and advancing educational equity for Boston youth.

Tenacity provides equal employment opportunities without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, marital status, veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity, or genetic information. Individuals from underrepresented groups are strongly encouraged to apply.
